notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → waiting when the gemini turn ends with a clarifying question. Unlike claudecode/codex, gemini-cli has NO user-blocking tool — the waiting episode comes purely from the agent-agnostic trailing-? heuristic: a final text-only 'gemini' message is inferred as turn_done (parser.go:209), IsAgentDone fires (primary path), and IsWaitingForUserInput sees the literal '?' in LastAssistantText → state_classifier rule 2a emits working→waiting. The recipe deliberately stops at the waiting episode and does NOT send a follow-up reply; the waiting→working→ready return arc is out of scope. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE gemini-cli driver (gemini -y under tmux: send + wait_turn + 8s trailing sleep), NOT headless gemini -p, which exits at turn completion before the daemon observes the waiting edge. turn_end_waiting anchors to turn_start so the matcher picks the post-turn waiting, not session birth. observations.model (gemini-3.5-flash) is the live-recorded default boot model on the API-key tier (record settled it from the provisional gemini-3-flash-preview). The whole verdict is emission-dependent: it assumes the reply contains a literal '?'.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch

invariants: no state_transition to ready after waiting
text: Final text-only 'gemini' reply ending in '?' is inferred turn_done; IsWaitingForUserInput sees the literal '?' → state_classifier rule 2a emits working → waiting. Final state = waiting.
